prompt:
The postage-meter crate is refusing to build and I half suspect the compiler is wrong, which I know means it isn't, but look at this. Two errors. The first is plain enough:

  error: expected `,` following `match` arm
    --> src/lib.rs:22:29

The second one is the one melting my brain (trimmed the wall of pipes out of the middle):

  error[E0308]: `match` arms have incompatible types
    --> src/lib.rs:37:27
    ...
    Zone::Regional => 80 + (weight_g / 250) * 10,
                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ expected `()`, found `u32`

Expected UNIT?? The Regional arm is character-for-character the same shape as the National arm right under it, and it's been charging the right regional fee since spring. Why would rustc decide that one arm of a fee table ought to be `()`?

The laminated rate card is law: base rates per class, the per-band distance fees, the 900-cent cap, the roll-line format — tests/postrate.rs pins every number and cargo test --offline has to come back green. Fix the build with the smallest diff possible, do not "improve" the rate logic while you're there, and tests/, Cargo.toml, and .gitignore stay untouched.
